review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to dynamic reconfiguration in response to incidents, which is feasible through admission controllers, audit logging, network policies, and RBAC. The original function names were mostly clear but required slight adjustments for consistency and clarity. The changes ensure that the function names better reflect the dynamic nature of the reconfiguration in response to incidents.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the dynamic nature of the reconfiguration in response to incidents.
title: Incident Handling | Dynamic Reconfiguration
step1_reasoning: The control IR-4 (2) focuses on dynamic reconfiguration in response to incidents, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that the cluster can dynamically adjust configurations in response to security incidents. This involves enabling dynamic reconfiguration capabilities through admission controllers, ensuring audit logs capture incident response activities, updating network policies dynamically upon threat detection, and revoking access dynamically through RBAC adjustments.
